extraction.one_line_summary: TRATNIK Jan soloed to victory from 28-rider breakaway after bridging on penultimate Monte Ragogna climb, holding off O'CONNOR's chase.
tactical_patterns: GC teams granted 28-rider break excessive freedom before Madonna di Campiglio, revealing strategic energy conservation before decisive mountain stage.
Tratnik attacked with 40km remaining on penultimate Monte Ragogna lap, not final climb, exploiting organizational chaos in oversized break.
Almeida gained 2 seconds in final kilometer on flat finish against GC rivals, signaling unusual time trial strength or rival weakness.

rider_signals.TRATNIK Jan: positive: managed 40-second solo effort perfectly after O'Connor bridge, suggesting exceptional power reserve after 6-hour breakaway effort
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: closed 40 seconds to Tratnik on penultimate climb at age 24, demonstrating grand tour breakaway capacity despite second place
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: extended pink jersey lead to 17 seconds by gapping GC rivals in final kilometer of flat finish, unusual aggression
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: negative: lost 2 seconds to Almeida in final kilometer sprint despite being stronger time trialist, reveals poor positioning or legs
rider_signals.VISCONTI Giovanni: neutral: defended mountains jersey passively in 28-rider break, lost only 1 point despite Guerreiro's attacks
